questions.question_text: A Georgia licensee verbally promises a buyer that a property's value will double within two years. Which unfair practice is this?
questions.explanation: O.C.G.A. 43-40-25 prohibits licensees from guaranteeing future profits on real estate. Promising a property will double in value is a clear violation because no one can guarantee market performance.

questions.question_text: A Georgia agent drafts a custom legal clause for a purchase contract at the buyer's request. What violation has occurred?
questions.explanation: Georgia licensees may fill in the blanks of form contracts prepared by an attorney (such as GAR forms) but may not draft custom legal clauses or documents — that is the unauthorized practice of law under Georgia's practice-of-law statutes (O.C.G.A. 15-19-51), even if the client requests it. It is not one of the enumerated unfair practices in O.C.G.A. 43-40-25(b), but it still exposes the licensee to legal liability and potential discipline.

questions.question_text: An agent collects rent on behalf of an owner and deposits the funds into her personal checking account. Which unfair practice applies?
questions.explanation: Depositing client funds into a personal account is commingling, which is strictly prohibited under O.C.G.A. 43-40-25. Client funds must always remain separate from a licensee's personal or business operating funds.

questions.question_text: A listing agent contacts a buyer who is already under contract with another agent and encourages the buyer to cancel that contract. This is BEST described as which violation?
questions.explanation: Encouraging a party to break an existing contract to gain a new client is inducing breach of contract, an unfair practice under O.C.G.A. 43-40-25. The agent should respect existing contractual obligations between parties.

questions.question_text: A broker proposes a net listing where the seller receives a fixed amount and the broker keeps any excess proceeds. How does Georgia law treat net listings?
questions.correct_answer: They are prohibited by Georgia Real Estate Commission rule
distractors: They are legal and commonly used in Georgia
They are permitted only for commercial property
They are legal if the seller consents in writing
questions.explanation: GREC Substantive Regulation 520-1-.06(1)(c) prohibits brokers from accepting net brokerage engagements: the broker must add the brokerage fee to the seller's desired net so the client knows the gross price of the property and the broker's services. Net listings are not merely discouraged — accepting one violates Commission rules and subjects the licensee to discipline. The ban comes from Commission regulation; net listings are not among the unfair practices enumerated in O.C.G.A. 43-40-25(b).

questions.question_text: A homeowner pays an unlicensed friend $500 to help negotiate the sale of her investment property. Who faces potential license law violations?
questions.correct_answer: Only the unlicensed friend who performed the act
distractors: Both the homeowner and the unlicensed friend
Only the homeowner who authorized payment
Neither, because the amount is under $1,000
questions.explanation: Under O.C.G.A. 43-40-30, performing even a single licensed act — such as negotiating the sale of another person's property for compensation — without a license is unlawful. The friend faces cease-and-desist orders, fines, and misdemeanor charges (O.C.G.A. 43-40-31), and cannot sue to collect the $500 (O.C.G.A. 43-40-24). The homeowner violates nothing: owners are exempt for their own property (O.C.G.A. 43-40-29), and only licensees are prohibited from compensating unlicensed persons (O.C.G.A. 43-40-25(b)(17)).

questions.question_text: An agent places an online advertisement for a property that does not include the broker's name or firm identification. What type of violation is this?
questions.correct_answer: A blind ad without broker identification
questions.explanation: Georgia license law requires all advertising to include the broker's or firm's identification. An ad that omits this information is considered a blind ad and violates license law, regardless of the advertising medium used.

questions.question_text: After a buyer signs a purchase agreement, the listing agent fails to provide a copy of the contract to the buyer. Which obligation has the agent breached?
questions.correct_answer: Providing contract copies to all parties
distractors: Recording the contract at the courthouse
Depositing earnest money within five days
questions.explanation: Georgia license law requires that copies of all signed contracts be provided to every party involved. Failing to do so is a license law violation, separate from any earnest money or recording obligations.

questions.question_text: A licensed salesperson allows her unlicensed assistant to negotiate repair credits directly with the buyer on her behalf. Which violation applies?
questions.correct_answer: Allowing unlicensed persons to perform licensed acts
distractors: Failing to maintain a definite place of business
questions.explanation: Negotiating terms of a transaction is a licensed activity in Georgia. Permitting an unlicensed assistant to negotiate on behalf of a licensee is a violation of Georgia license law, regardless of the licensee's supervision.

questions.question_text: A salesperson whose license has expired continues to show property and write offers for two weeks before renewing. What is the MOST serious violation?
questions.correct_answer: Practicing real estate without a valid license
distractors: Failing to complete continuing education hours
Operating under an inactive broker relationship
questions.explanation: Performing licensed activities such as showing property and writing offers while a license is expired constitutes practicing without a license. This is one of the most serious violations under Georgia license law and can result in disciplinary action and fines.

questions.question_text: Which Georgia agency is responsible for investigating fair housing complaints filed under the Georgia Fair Housing Act?
questions.explanation: The Georgia Commission on Equal Opportunity (GCEO) is the state agency responsible for investigating complaints under the GA Fair Housing Act (O.C.G.A. 8-3-200). GREC handles license law matters, not fair housing enforcement.

questions.question_text: A seller instructs her Georgia agent to reject all offers from families with children. What should the agent do?
questions.correct_answer: Refuse and explain this violates fair housing law
distractors: Follow the instruction as the seller's fiduciary
Comply only if the property qualifies as senior housing
Report the seller directly to local police
questions.explanation: Familial status is a protected class under both federal and Georgia fair housing law. Agents may NOT follow discriminatory instructions from clients, even when acting as their fiduciary. The agent must refuse and educate the seller about fair housing requirements.

questions.question_text: Under the Georgia Fair Housing Act, within what time period must an administrative complaint be filed with the GCEO?
questions.correct_answer: One year from the alleged violation
distractors: Two years from the alleged violation
Six months from the alleged violation
questions.explanation: Administrative complaints under the Georgia Fair Housing Act must be filed with the GCEO within one year of the alleged discriminatory act. A separate private civil action may be filed within two years, but the administrative deadline is one year.

questions.question_text: An owner-occupied duplex owner in Georgia refuses to rent the other unit based on a tenant's religion. A Georgia agent assists with the refusal. Is the agent protected by the Mrs. Murphy exemption?
questions.correct_answer: No, agents may never follow discriminatory instructions
distractors: Yes, the exemption covers agents acting for exempt owners
Yes, owner-occupied duplexes are fully exempt
Only if the owner provides written authorization
questions.explanation: While the Mrs. Murphy exemption may shield an owner-occupied property with four or fewer units from certain fair housing claims, a licensed agent may never participate in or assist with housing discrimination. The exemption does not extend to licensees acting on the owner's behalf.

questions.question_text: A buyer wants to file a private civil lawsuit for housing discrimination in Georgia. What is the deadline for filing?
questions.correct_answer: Two years from the discriminatory act
distractors: One year from the discriminatory act
Three years from the discriminatory act
questions.explanation: Under the Georgia Fair Housing Act, a private civil action must be filed within two years of the alleged discriminatory act. This is separate from the one-year deadline for filing an administrative complaint with the GCEO.
